is one of the [106 constituencies](/wiki/List_of_constituencies_of_Hungary "List of constituencies of Hungary") into which the territory of Hungary is divided by Act CCIII of 2011, and in which voters can [elect](/wiki/Electoral_system_of_Hungary "Electoral system of Hungary") one member of the [National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_(Hungary) "National Assembly (Hungary)"). The standard abbreviation of the constituency name is: *Somogy 03. OEVK*. The seat is [Marcali](/wiki/Marcali "Marcali").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Constituency in Hungary (2014–)

| Somogy County 3rd constituency | |
| --- | --- |

| Elected | [2014](/wiki/2014_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2014 Hungarian parliamentary election"), [2018](/wiki/2018_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2018 Hungarian parliamentary election"), [2022](/wiki/2022_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2022 Hungarian parliamentary election") |

The **Somogy County 3rd parliamentary constituency** is one of the [106 constituencies](/wiki/List_of_constituencies_of_Hungary "List of constituencies of Hungary") into which the territory of Hungary is divided by Act CCIII of 2011, and in which voters can [elect](/wiki/Electoral_system_of_Hungary "Electoral system of Hungary") one member of the [National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_(Hungary) "National Assembly (Hungary)"). The standard abbreviation of the constituency name is: *Somogy 03. OEVK*. The seat is [Marcali](/wiki/Marcali "Marcali").

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Somogy_County_3rd_constituency&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Demographics")]

Historical population

| Year | Pop. | ±% |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2011 | 79,567 | — |
| 2022 | 75,062 | −5.7% |

The demographics of the constituency are as follows.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) The population of constituency No. 3 of Somogy County was 75,062 on 1 October 2022. The population of the constituency decreased by 4,505 people between the 2011 and 2022 censuses. Based on the age composition, the majority of the population in the constituency is middle-aged with 27,282 people, while the least is children with 12,697 people. 79.3% of the population of the constituency has internet access.

According to the highest level of completed education, those with a high school diploma are the most numerous, with 18,762 people, followed by skilled workers with 17,387 people.

According to economic activity, almost half of the population is employed, 32,335 people, the second most significant group is inactive earners, who are mainly pensioners, with 22,028 people.

The most significant ethnic group in the constituency is [German](/wiki/Germans "Germans") with 2,404 people and [Romani](/wiki/Romani_people "Romani people") with 2,018 people. The proportion of foreign citizens without Hungarian citizenship is 4.2%.

According to religious composition, the largest religion of the residents of the constituency is Roman Catholic (27,236 people), and a significant community is the Calvinist (2,501 people). The number of those not belonging to a religious community is also significant (6,098 people), the second largest group in the constituency after the Roman Catholic religion.
